signal_title: Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act 2024
risk_rationale: The new Australian legislation mandates a minimum age of 16 for social media accounts, conflicting with TikTok's current minimum registration age of 13. This represents a material compliance gap requiring immediate product changes to avoid potential fines or restrictions in the Australian market.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Minimum registration age is 13 years globally.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Prevent individuals under 16 from creating accounts.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The new legislation requires a higher minimum age for account creation than TikTok's current policy.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: No specific age verification measures mandated.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The legislation mandates age verification measures which are not currently specified in TikTok's baseline.

remediations.title: Implement Age Verification for Australian Users
remediations.description: Develop and integrate an age verification system for users in Australia to comply with the new minimum age requirement of 16.
acceptance_criteria: Age verification system is implemented and operational for all new Australian accounts.
Users under 16 are prevented from creating accounts in Australia.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new legislation could result in significant fines or restrictions on TikTok's operations in Australia, impacting user growth and market presence.
recommended_actions: Initiate development of an age verification system tailored for the Australian market.
Update the registration process to enforce a minimum age of 16 for Australian users.
Engage with Australian regulatory bodies to ensure compliance and address any potential concerns.
